meaning: n.因循守旧,陈旧古板的做法,前后紧接,步伐一致
examples: After years of moving in lockstep, France and Germany are growing apart at an alarming rate.
The coffin was carried by six soldiers walking in lockstep.
Cases of breathing difficulties increase in lockstep with air pollution.
Stocks have been rising since 1982, and through 1992 they rose almost in lockstep with earnings.
She might be our heroine, yet her liberation and her corruption work in lockstep.
